techniques.name: Phishing
techniques.description: Adversaries send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems, typically via malicious attachments or links.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing software to gain initial access to a network.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries leverage external-facing remote services (VPN, RDP, Citrix) to initially access a network.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts to gain initial access, persistence, or privilege escalation.

techniques.name: Trusted Relationship
techniques.description: Adversaries breach organizations via trusted third-party relationships that have privileged access to the target network.

techniques.name: Supply Chain Compromise
techniques.description: Adversaries manipulate products or delivery mechanisms prior to receipt by a final consumer to compromise data or systems.

techniques.name: Drive-by Compromise
techniques.description: Adversaries gain access through a user visiting a compromised website during normal browsing.

techniques.name: Hardware Additions
techniques.description: Adversaries introduce rogue hardware (USB devices, network implants) to gain access to systems.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.

techniques.name: Exploitation for Client Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries exploit software vulnerabilities in client applications to execute code on target systems.

techniques.name: User Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries rely on a user executing a malicious file or link to gain execution on the system.

techniques.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) to execute malicious commands and payloads.

techniques.name: Scheduled Task/Job
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse task scheduling functionality to facilitate initial or recurring execution of malicious code.

techniques.name: System Services
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse system services or daemons to execute commands or programs.

techniques.name: Native API
techniques.description: Adversaries interact directly with the native OS API to execute behaviors, bypassing higher-level monitoring.

techniques.name: Inter-Process Communication
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse inter-process communication mechanisms (COM, DDE) to execute commands on local or remote systems.

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: Adversaries encrypt data on target systems to interrupt availability, typically for ransom.

techniques.name: Data Destruction
techniques.description: Adversaries destroy data and files on specific systems or across a network to interrupt availability.

techniques.name: Inhibit System Recovery
techniques.description: Adversaries delete or remove built-in OS data and disable services designed to aid in system recovery.

techniques.name: Service Stop
techniques.description: Adversaries stop or disable services on a system to render those services unavailable to legitimate users.

techniques.name: Defacement
techniques.description: Adversaries modify visual content to deliver messaging, intimidate, or claim credit for an intrusion.

techniques.name: Disk Wipe
techniques.description: Adversaries wipe or corrupt raw disk data on specific systems or across a network to interrupt availability.

techniques.name: System Shutdown/Reboot
techniques.description: Adversaries shut down or reboot systems to interrupt access or aid in destruction of those systems.

techniques.name: Network Denial of Service
techniques.description: Adversaries perform network denial of service to degrade or block availability of targeted resources.
